Dice.label: D3 — Triangular Prism
Dice.description: A triangular prism used as a three-sided die when rolled on its rectangular faces. It is one of the simplest prism-based dice.

Dice.label: D4 — Tetrahedron
Dice.description: The classic four-sided die. It is one of the five Platonic solids, with all faces equal equilateral triangles.

Dice.label: D6 — Cube (Hexahedron)
Dice.description: The familiar six-sided die. The cube is the most common gaming die and one of the Platonic solids.

Dice.label: D8 — Octahedron
Dice.description: An eight-sided die with triangular faces. The octahedron is the dual of the cube.

Dice.label: D10 — Pentagonal Trapezohedron
Dice.description: The standard ten-sided die used in many RPG systems. Its kite-shaped faces make it distinct from the Platonic and Archimedean solids.

Dice.label: D12 — Dodecahedron
Dice.description: A twelve-sided die with pentagonal faces. It is one of the five Platonic solids and the dual of the icosahedron.

Dice.label: D20 — Icosahedron
Dice.description: The iconic twenty-sided die. It is a Platonic solid with 20 triangular faces and is the dual of the dodecahedron.

Dice.label: D100 — Percentile Die
Dice.description: Usually the same geometry as a D10, but numbered by tens from 00 to 90 for percentile rolls.

Polyhedra.label: Tetrahedron
Polyhedra.description: The simplest convex polyhedron. Every face is an equilateral triangle.

Polyhedra.label: Cube
Polyhedra.description: Also called a hexahedron. Its square faces make it a useful baseline for many geometric constructions.

Polyhedra.label: Octahedron
Polyhedra.description: A Platonic solid made from eight equilateral triangles. It is the dual of the cube.

Polyhedra.label: Dodecahedron
Polyhedra.description: A Platonic solid with twelve pentagonal faces. It has a strong visual connection to fantasy dice and classic sacred geometry.

Polyhedra.label: Icosahedron
Polyhedra.description: A Platonic solid with twenty triangular faces. It provides one of the most spherical-looking forms among the regular solids.

Polyhedra.label: Truncated Icosahedron
Polyhedra.description: Famous as the classic soccer-ball pattern, with 12 pentagons and 20 hexagons. It is also the structure associated with buckminsterfullerene.

Polyhedra.label: Pentagonal Trapezohedron
Polyhedra.description: A trapezohedron with ten congruent kite-shaped faces meeting at two apex vertices. Unlike the Platonic solids here, its faces are not regular polygons.

Prisms & Antiprisms.label: Triangular Prism
Prisms & Antiprisms.description: The simplest prism, made from two triangles joined by three rectangles. It is also commonly used as a D3.

Prisms & Antiprisms.label: Pentagonal Prism
Prisms & Antiprisms.description: A prism built from two pentagons connected by five rectangles. Good for exploring n-gonal prism families.

Prisms & Antiprisms.label: Hexagonal Prism
Prisms & Antiprisms.description: A six-sided prism that extends the same pattern as the triangular and pentagonal prisms. Useful in honeycomb-inspired forms.

Prisms & Antiprisms.label: Octagonal Prism
Prisms & Antiprisms.description: An eight-sided prism with a more rounded visual feel. It is a nice bridge between polygonal and near-cylindrical forms.

Prisms & Antiprisms.label: Square Antiprism
Prisms & Antiprisms.description: Built from two squares rotated relative to each other and connected by triangles. It is the simplest true antiprism.

Prisms & Antiprisms.label: Pentagonal Antiprism
Prisms & Antiprisms.description: A five-sided antiprism with a nice twisted, energetic look. It is often more visually dynamic than the matching prism.

Prisms & Antiprisms.label: Hexagonal Antiprism
Prisms & Antiprisms.description: A six-sided antiprism with a strong geometric rhythm. It is useful for exploring twist-based form generation.

Archimedean Solids.label: Truncated Tetrahedron
Archimedean Solids.description: Created by slicing the corners off a tetrahedron. It has 4 triangles and 4 hexagons.

Archimedean Solids.label: Cuboctahedron
Archimedean Solids.description: An Archimedean solid with both square and triangular faces. It is one of the cleanest examples of a uniform mixed-face solid.

Archimedean Solids.label: Truncated Cube
Archimedean Solids.description: Formed by cutting the corners off a cube. It has 8 triangles and 6 octagons.

Archimedean Solids.label: Truncated Octahedron
Archimedean Solids.description: A uniform solid with 8 hexagons and 6 squares. It can tessellate space, which makes it especially interesting for 3D packing.

Archimedean Solids.label: Rhombicuboctahedron
Archimedean Solids.description: An Archimedean solid with 18 squares and 8 triangles. Its name is intimidating, but its face pattern is very orderly.

Archimedean Solids.label: Icosidodecahedron
Archimedean Solids.description: A balanced blend of the icosahedron and dodecahedron, with both triangles and pentagons.

Archimedean Solids.label: Truncated Dodecahedron
Archimedean Solids.description: Made by truncating a dodecahedron. It has 12 decagons and 20 triangles.

Archimedean Solids.label: Truncated Icosahedron
Archimedean Solids.description: Famous as the classic soccer-ball pattern, with 12 pentagons and 20 hexagons. It is also the structure associated with buckminsterfullerene.

Catalan / Dual Solids.label: Rhombic Dodecahedron
Catalan / Dual Solids.description: The dual of the cuboctahedron. It fills space without gaps, making it important in tessellation and crystallography.

Catalan / Dual Solids.label: Triakis Tetrahedron
Catalan / Dual Solids.description: The dual of the truncated tetrahedron. It looks like a tetrahedron reinforced with triangular spikes.

Catalan / Dual Solids.label: Tetrakis Hexahedron
Catalan / Dual Solids.description: The dual of the truncated octahedron. It can be visualized as a cube with a pyramid added to each face.

Catalan / Dual Solids.label: Rhombic Triacontahedron
Catalan / Dual Solids.description: The dual of the icosidodecahedron. It has 30 rhombic faces and a very rich, faceted appearance.

Experimental.label: Stellated Octahedron
Experimental.description: Also known as the stella octangula. It can be seen as two interpenetrating tetrahedra and has a strong star-like silhouette.
